data.title: PR comment response: accept/defend/defer for engineer-authored PR review comments
data.description:
## Context
After epic 1083-fb3d shifts the merge workflow from direct push to PR-based delivery, AI agents creating PRs have no mechanism to respond to engineer review comments. Comments on both inline code review threads and top-level PR conversation threads go unaddressed, stalling PRs until an engineer manually intervenes or the agent fails to apply valid feedback. This epic adds a systematic response loop — mirroring the accept/defend/defer taxonomy from plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md — adapted for engineer-authored natural-language comments rather than structured reviewer findings. The standalone and merge-integrated entry points share one underlying script, ensuring consistent behavior.

## Success Criteria
1. pr-comment-response.sh fetches all unresolved PR comments (both inline and top-level conversation comments) via paginated gh api calls, and writes them to a normalized JSON file with schema {comment_id, root_comment_id, thread_url, body, author, is_inline, already_replied, defense_comment_id}; root_comment_id is the top-level comment in the thread (resolved by walking in_reply_to_id to the true root for replies); defense_comment_id is null on initial fetch; comments are excluded when the thread contains a reply beginning with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->; when no unresolved comments exist, exits 0 and writes {"comments": [], "skipped_count": 0}; exits non-zero with ERROR: on API failure
2. For each comment classified as accept: a general-purpose fix sub-agent receives the comment body, relevant source files, and current diff; if the sub-agent signals multi-file scope, returns no changes, or reports a conflict, the orchestrator reverts any partial working-tree changes and reclassifies to defer per SC4; on clean success, commits and pushes and posts a reply referencing the commit SHA; all reply bodies begin with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
3. For each comment classified as defend: step 1 — post a new PR review comment (not a # REVIEW-DEFENSE: source code comment) via the correct endpoint (inline: POST /pulls/{n}/comments/{root_comment_id}/replies; top-level: POST /issues/{n}/comments) and write the returned comment ID to defense_comment_id in the normalized JSON; step 2 — reply to the originating thread linking to that review comment; on retry, defense_comment_id being non-null skips step 1 and proceeds directly to step 2; all reply bodies begin with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
4. For each comment classified as defer: creates a tracking ticket, writes the ticket ID to the normalized JSON before any API reply call, and posts a reply with the ticket ID and brief rationale; reply body begins with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
5. merge-to-main.sh gains a comment_response phase inserted after CI passes and before the merge phase; the phase processes a fixed snapshot captured at phase entry; when accept-classified comments produce new commits, CI polling re-runs via the existing gh pr checks loop against the updated HEAD SHA before merge; --resume correctly positions the phase in the existing sequence, including correct behavior when an existing state file predates the phase addition
6. A /dso:respond-to-pr-comments standalone skill processes open PR comments on demand; accepts a PR URL or number; when no unresolved comments exist, prints "No unresolved comments found on PR #<n>."; otherwise prints a summary table of comment ID, classification, and action taken
7. Comment response autonomy is configurable via pr_comments.human_in_loop in dso-config.conf (default: false; absent key = false); when true, proposes each classification before acting; detects CI=true or no TTY and falls back to autonomous with a warning; superseded by 7ac3-71a1 generalized pattern if that epic ships first
8. The orchestrator classifies each comment using an embedded prompt in the skill SKILL.md (no separate classifier sub-agent); maps engineer comment intent to accept (actionable requested change), defend (design decision justifiable with codebase evidence), or defer (out-of-scope, ambiguous, or blocked); ambiguous comments default to defer
9. In the integration test suite, a mock GitHub API fixture provides a PR with one accept, one defend, and one defer comment (including a reply-in-thread to exercise root_comment_id chain-walk); the mock is scoped so no real gh api calls escape; after the phase runs, all three reply calls are verified, the accept fix commit is present, the defer ticket ID is in the JSON, and defense_comment_id is non-null for the defend comment

## Dependencies
- 1083-fb3d (PR-based merge workflow) must be completed first; the comment_response phase in merge-to-main.sh requires the PR-based architecture that epic establishes
- 3aaa-0238 (CI-enforced quality gates) soft dependency: SC5 CI re-polling reuses the existing gh pr checks signal; if 3aaa-0238 changes the CI status shape, the re-poll may need updating
- 7ac3-71a1 (Optional human review steps) relates_to: SC7 pr_comments.human_in_loop key will be superseded by the generalized pattern from 7ac3-71a1 if that epic ships first; coordinate to avoid duplicate config key namespaces

## Approach
A new pr-comment-response.sh script handles all GitHub API I/O for a single run using one API layer throughout (either GitHub MCP server tools or gh api — not mixed). Preferred: GitHub MCP server (github/github-mcp-server v0.31.0+) if configured and all required tools are available (probed at startup); fallback triggered on any tool failure (401, 403, tool-not-found, timeout). Fallback: gh api --paginate with explicit endpoint routing by is_inline. The orchestrator classifies via embedded SKILL.md prompt, dispatches a general-purpose fix sub-agent for accept, posts directly for defend and defer. root_comment_id resolved at fetch time by walking in_reply_to_id chain to true root. defense_comment_id written after step 1 of defend. Ticket IDs written to JSON before reply calls. Partial working-tree changes reverted before reclassify-to-defer. Parallel implementation path from REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md R1-R5.

## Scenario Analysis
- Race condition: CI re-poll targets wrong HEAD SHA (critical, runtime): CI re-poll must verify check SHA matches current branch HEAD after accept commits.
- ci_trigger phase ordering conflict (critical, deployment): Phase order must guarantee comment_response + CI re-poll fully completes before merge phase.
- MCP server configured but tool names differ from v0.31.0 (high, runtime): Fallback to gh api must trigger on tool-not-found, not just server-not-configured.
- MCP server authenticated but token expired (high, configuration): Fallback must trigger on 401/403 from MCP calls.
- MCP vs. gh api use different comment ID namespaces (high, runtime): Use single API layer per run; do not mix MCP and gh api within one script invocation.
- Defend two-step: step 1 succeeds, step 2 fails (high, runtime): defense_comment_id written to JSON after step 1 prevents step-1 duplication on retry (SC3).
- Accept: multi-file comment scope misinterpreted (high, runtime): Fix sub-agent signals multi-file scope; orchestrator reclassifies to defer with revert (SC2).
- Accept: partial working-tree change after reclassify-to-defer (high, runtime): Orchestrator reverts all working-tree changes before reclassifying to defer (SC2).
- Sentinel deduplication fails on GitHub truncation or edit (high, runtime)
- Pagination silently drops comments beyond page 1 (high, runtime): gh api --paginate required.
- Concurrent merge runs double-process same comments (high, runtime)
- Fix sub-agent pushes to stale branch HEAD (high, runtime): Non-fast-forward triggers reclassify-to-defer.
- State file schema version not bumped for new phase (high, deployment): SC5 --resume requirement covers phase insertion compatibility.
- GITHUB_TOKEN lacks write permissions (high, deployment): SC1 ERROR: prefix on API failure.
- human_in_loop=true deadlocks in non-interactive CI (high, configuration): SC7 TTY/CI detection fallback.
- Sentinel string breaks JSON if shell-interpolated (high, configuration)
- root_comment_id wrong for outdated/re-anchored comments (medium, runtime): Fallback to POST /issues/{n}/comments when inline reply returns 422.
- Reply-to-reply 422: chain walk stops one step too early (medium, runtime): Walk until in_reply_to_id is null.
- Defer creates duplicate tickets on retry (medium, runtime): Ticket ID written before reply call (SC4).
- Wrong reply endpoint for inline vs. top-level (medium, runtime): SC3 endpoint selection by is_inline.
- Engineers comment is a reply in existing thread (medium, runtime): root_comment_id resolution in SC1.
- GitHub API rate limit exhausted mid-batch (medium, runtime): Rate-limit detection and back-off.
- gh CLI version lacks --paginate on older self-hosted runners (medium, deployment): Minimum gh CLI version check at script startup.
- Defend for comment on deleted file returns 422 (medium, runtime): Fallback to POST /issues/{n}/comments.
- Integration test fixture IDs collide if mock boundary leaks (medium, runtime): Mock must enforce no-real-calls assertion.
